page: Google Cloud Platform ct3p-hightpu-4t is an accelerator-optimized server belonging to the ct3p family. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 340.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.08 GB of RAM per core. The system is configured as a single vNUMA node.

The primary hardware feature of this server is the integration of four ct3p accelerators. This configuration does not include any local NVMe or SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

An analysis of the CPU flags reveals that this instance lacks standard x86 vector and matrix extensions, such as AVX, AVX-512, and AMX, as well as hardware-assisted cryptography and virtualization extensions. This indicates that heavy CPU-bound vector math or nested virtualization tasks are not supported, shifting the computational focus entirely onto the dedicated ct3p accelerators.

Given the presence of four ct3p accelerators and 340.0 GB of RAM, this server is designed for machine learning workloads, specifically TPU-targeted training and inference tasks. The high memory capacity supports large datasets in memory, while the dedicated CPU allocation ensures stable host-to-accelerator communication. From a resource density perspective, the lack of local storage and advanced CPU instruction sets represents a design tradeoff that optimizes cost efficiency for workloads that offload mathematical computations to the specialized accelerators.
description: Google Cloud Platform ct3p-hightpu-4t is an accelerator-optimized server designed for specialized machine learning and parallel computing workloads. It features 48 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs, 340.0 GB of RAM (7.08 GB per core), and a single vNUMA node configuration. The server is equipped with four ct3p accelerators to handle intensive parallel processing tasks. Notably, the instance lacks local storage and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Technical tradeoffs include the absence of standard CPU instruction extensions such as AVX, AMX, and hardware cryptography flags, meaning computational tasks must rely heavily on the integrated accelerators rather than the host CPU. This resource profile is highly optimized for TPU-driven model training, deep learning inference, and data pipeline processing where local storage is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform ct3p-hightpu-4t is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs, 340 GB RAM, and four ct3p accelerators, designed for machine learning and parallel processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform ct3p-hightpu-4t features 48 dedicated vCPUs, 340 GB RAM, and four ct3p accelerators for machine learning workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized host featuring four specialized processors and dedicated memory for high-throughput machine learning and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Four integrated ct3p accelerators for parallel computing and machine learning workloads.
48 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs with 340.0 GB of system memory.
Single vNUMA node configuration with 7.08 GB of RAM per core.
No local storage included, requiring network-attached storage solutions.
Lacks standard CPU vector extensions like AVX and AMX.
